sections.strategy: John Lewis Partnership opened a redundancy consultation around 8-9 July 2026 covering roughly 200 store roles, proposing to close bureau de change desks in 30 stores and dedicated gift-wrapping counters in 25 stores from this autumn, with gift wrapping moving to till points instead. JLP frames it as modernising services to match changing customer behaviour (more travel money bought online), and it sits alongside the wider £800m store-transformation programme that is instead pouring money into personal styling, hospitality and flagship refits like Glasgow.
sections.gossip: The 200-role consultation drew union criticism and press coverage noting it follows roughly 3,300 roles cut the year before, raising questions about how JLP squares service cuts with the 2% Partnership Bonus and record store investment it announced earlier this year.
soWhat: No new vendor, certification, or sourcing news broke this week — a quiet window suppliers can use to confirm GOTS/RWS/OEKO-TEX renewal status ahead of JLP's next buying cycle rather than waiting to be asked.

The gift-wrap/bureau de change closures show JLP trimming low-margin, transactional in-store services to redirect labour and capital toward experience-led retail (styling, hospitality, flagship refits) — suppliers whose product supports those premium, service-heavy formats are better aligned with where JLP is investing than suppliers of commodity add-ons.

With no fresh collection or brand-partner news this week, JLP's fashion pipeline looks to be mid-cycle between its SS26 rollout (LABRUM, Mulberry, 100 new brands) and an AW26 push — expect the next wave of buyer briefs and supplier outreach in late summer.
